title: The HP-Compaq Merger: If There's the Will, There's a Way
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Aberdeen InSight dated September 6, 2001 analyzing the proposed HP-Compaq merger. Kastner identifies customer concern about inward focus, product-line redundancy across servers, Unix, and services, and urges outward customer focus as the key to success. Warns that Dell, IBM, and Sun will benefit if HP-Compaq loses customer focus during integration.
importance_rationale: Documents one of the largest tech mergers of the era ($25B) at the time of announcement; Kastner's analysis captures market sentiment and identifies key risks.
relevance_rationale: Kastner-authored Aberdeen InSight on landmark HP-Compaq merger; exemplifies Kastner's M&A analysis methodology.
prescience_rationale: Accurately predicted integration challenges, customer communication failures, and benefit to Dell/IBM/Sun; post-merger outcomes largely confirmed concerns.
